summary: End-to-end verification of the Nexus Panel env-var delivery bug fix. All target features PASS: (1) superadmin login, (2) creating a project with env_vars and triggering deploy writes /opt/nexus-panel/apps/<slug>/backend/.env containing JWT_SECRET=supersecret123, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=sk-abc, MONGO_URL=..., DB_NAME=envtest2_db, CORS_ORIGINS=... AND docker-compose.yml has the same vars under backend.environment, (3) PUT /api/projects/{id} with env_vars persists and is returned by GET, (4) UI Environment editor on ProjectDetail saves JWT_SECRET=mysecret and shows 'Configuration saved' toast; GET reflects it, (5) Regression endpoints (root /api/, ssl-status, system/containers-health, terminal commands, delete cleanup) all return 200. 6/6 pytest tests pass.
critical_code_review_comments: deploy_engine.backend_env() correctly writes MONGO_URL/DB_NAME/CORS_ORIGINS plus every user env var. _write_artifacts guards on backend/frontend dir existence so it's tolerant of unusual repo layouts.
compose_yaml() emits the env vars under the backend service environment block as expected — duplicated source of truth is fine because Emergent apps can read either .env file or process env.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral projects (envtest2, envpersist, sslreg, uienv) — all deleted at test teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Env-var delivery bug fix is fully verified. /tmp/testrepo (local git repo main branch, with backend/ + frontend/) is present and reusable. Deploy will always fail at the docker step in the sandbox (expected: Docker unavailable) — artifact files are written before that and inspected under /opt/nexus-panel/apps/<slug>/. pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_env_delivery.py covers the full flow.
rca of the issue: Original bug: cloned repo lacked backend/.env (gitignored) so deployed backend threw KeyError: 'JWT_SECRET'. Fix: deploy_engine._write_artifacts now writes backend/.env from backend_env(p) whenever a backend/ dir exists, containing MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, CORS_ORIGINS + all user-supplied env_vars; the same vars are also emitted under the backend service 'environment:' block in docker-compose.yml. Verified by cat'ing generated files after POST /api/projects/{id}/deploy.
